message:
fix(sources): correct ssh argv + detect renamed rsync --secluded-args flag

Closes #190, #191.

Two related sync-path bugs surfaced on macOS clients talking to existing
Linux remotes, where manual ssh/rsync against the same host+path worked
but cass commands reported the remote as missing.

#190 (sources doctor): `check_remote_path` passed `host, "sh", "-c", cmd`
as four separate argv elements to ssh(1). ssh joins post-host argv with
spaces before sending to the remote shell, which meant `sh -c test -d
'/path' && ls -1 '/path' | wc -l` reached the remote as a single line
where the remote shell — not `sh -c` — interpreted `&&` and `|`, and
`sh -c` only ever received the first token (`test`). Symptom: every
existing remote path reported "Path does not exist". Fix: pre-combine
`sh -c <single-arg>` into one post-host argv element so the quoted
script survives the round trip.

#191 (sources sync): `supports_protect_args` probed `rsync --help` for
the literal `--protect-args`, but rsync 3.4.0 renamed the primary form
to `--secluded-args` / `-s`, and current Homebrew `rsync 3.4.1` on macOS
prints only the new name. Detection returned false, code fell through
to the quoted-remote-spec branch, and rsync received paths shaped like
`host:'/path'`, which the remote side then tried to change-dir into
literally ("change_dir \"/home/user/'/home/user\" failed"). Replace
bool-only detection with a `RsyncArgProtection { None, ProtectArgs,
SecludedArgs }` enum that records both supported variants and hands the
caller the exact flag name its installed rsync accepted in --help.
`supports_protect_args()` keeps working as a backward-compat shim for
the WSL call site and the existing tests.

Regression tests cover enum-to-flag mapping and the pre-existing
remote-spec quoting semantics unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
